description:
67M post-renal-transplant, CRE K. pneumoniae bacteremia.
Meropenem MIC=8 (Resistant), ceftazidime-avibactam MIC=1 (Susceptible).
CrCl 40 mL/min (moderate impairment). No allergies.
citation: Tamma PD et al. IDSA 2022 Guidance on AMR Gram-Negative Infections. Clin Infect Dis. 2023;76(7):1228-1270. PMC9890506.
published_recommendation: Ceftazidime-avibactam (IDSA preferred for KPC-CRE bacteremia, renal-adjusted)

tool_results_log.result: Meropenem MIC = 8.0 mg/L -> EUCAST classification: Resistant (organism: K. pneumoniae).

IDSA recommendation for K. pneumoniae (ESBL) + bacteremia:
  First-line: ertapenem - 1g IV q24h for 14 days.
  Alternatives: meropenem.
  Notes: ESBL-producing organism. Carbapenems are drug of choice..

Renal function: CrCl 40.0 mL/min (moderate impairment).
Allergies reported: none.
Renal dosing alerts for drugs in this antibiogram:
  - meropenem: 1g IV q12h
  - ceftazidime-avibactam: 1.25g IV q8h
  - colistin: reduce maintenance dose
  - meropenem-vaborbactam: 2g IV q8h

description:
58M, MSSA bacteremia.
Oxacillin MIC=0.25 (Susceptible), cefazolin MIC=1 (Susceptible).
CrCl 65 mL/min. No allergies.
citation: Maraolo AE et al. Influence of Reported Penicillin Allergy on Mortality in MSSA Bacteremia. Open Forum Infect Dis. 2018;5(3):ofy042. doi:10.1093/ofid/ofy042.
published_recommendation: Cefazolin (preferred beta-lactam for penicillin-allergic patients with MSSA bacteremia; non-inferior to nafcillin in outcomes)

tool_results_log.result: Oxacillin MIC = 0.25 mg/L -> EUCAST classification: Susceptible (organism: S. aureus).

Renal function: CrCl 65.0 mL/min (normal / mild impairment).
Allergies reported: none.
Renal dosing alerts for drugs in this antibiogram:
  - oxacillin: 2g IV q4h
  - cefazolin: 2g IV q8h
  - vancomycin: 15-20mg/kg IV q8-12h
  - daptomycin: 6mg/kg IV q24h

IDSA recommendation for S. aureus (MSSA) + bacteremia:
  First-line: cefazolin - 2g IV q8h for 14-42 days depending on source and complications.
  Alternatives: nafcillin, oxacillin.
  Notes: MSSA bacteremia should receive an anti-staphylococcal beta-lactam when tolerated..

description:
72F on hemodialysis (CrCl 8 mL/min), VRE E. faecium bloodstream infection.
Vancomycin MIC=32 (Resistant), daptomycin MIC=1 (Susceptible), linezolid MIC=2 (Susceptible).
No allergies.
citation: Britt NS et al. Comparison of Effectiveness and Safety of Linezolid and Daptomycin in VRE Bloodstream Infection. Clin Infect Dis. 2015;61(6):871-878. PMC4551011.
published_recommendation: High-dose daptomycin (≥8 mg/kg, renal-adjusted, post-HD dosing); superior microbiologic clearance vs linezolid in VRE BSI

tool_results_log.result: Vancomycin MIC = 32.0 mg/L -> EUCAST classification: Resistant (organism: Enterococcus).

Renal function: CrCl 8.0 mL/min (kidney failure / dialysis-range).
Allergies reported: none.
Renal dosing alerts for drugs in this antibiogram:
  - vancomycin: 15-20mg/kg IV q48-72h
  - daptomycin: 8mg/kg IV post-HD
  - linezolid: 600mg IV/PO q12h
  - ampicillin: 2g IV q12-24h

IDSA recommendation for Enterococcus (VRE) + bacteremia:
  First-line: linezolid - 600mg IV/PO q12h for 7-14 days.
  Alternatives: daptomycin.
  Notes: Vancomycin-resistant Enterococcus. Use linezolid or daptomycin based on syndrome and susceptibility..
